summary: Python framework (v0.5.1) enabling LLM agents to control Android devices via natural language. Deployed as a tri-workspace component with Cursor/OpenClaw/Claude Desktop integration via MCP. Device: Samsung Galaxy S25 Ultra (SM-S938U, Android 16) connected over Tailscale (100.71.228.18:5555).
workspace_position: Third repo in tri-workspace: AI-Project-Manager orchestrates open--claw + droidrun. DroidRun is the phone control layer, exposing device automation as MCP tools to other AI clients.
responsibilities: Natural language Android device control via LLM agents
Portal APK lifecycle management (install, update, verify)
UI state reading (accessibility tree via Portal HTTP API)
Atomic device actions: tap, swipe, type, key, app launch, screenshot
Multi-provider LLM orchestration (DeepSeek, OpenRouter/Gemini, OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama, Google GenAI)
MCP server: exposing phone_do, phone_ping, phone_apps to AI clients
MCP client: consuming external MCP tools as agent capabilities
PowerShell automation: ADB lifecycle, API key injection from Bitwarden, startup
non_responsibilities: AI client UI (Cursor/OpenClaw/Claude Desktop own their UIs)
iOS production device control (experimental stub only)
Persistent user data storage between runs

architecture.style: event-driven LlamaIndex Workflows + MCP stdio server
layers: Layer 1: Portal APK on-device (HTTP:8080, Accessibility Service)
Layer 5: AI Clients (Cursor, OpenClaw, Claude Desktop)

entry_points: MCP server: python mcp_server.py (launched by start_mcp_server.ps1)
Python SDK: from droidrun import DroidAgent
startup_sequence: 1. startup_droidrun.ps1: inject API keys from Bitwarden Secrets Manager into env vars
3. start_mcp_server.ps1: launch mcp_server.py (done by AI client on demand)
5. LLM loaded via llm_loader (reads env vars for API keys)
7. AndroidDriver connects to device via ADB
8. PortalClient probes transport (TCP:8080 or content provider)
9. auto_setup check: Portal APK installed/updated if needed
10. Agent loop begins: observe UI -> LLM plan -> execute action (max 15 steps)
key_classes: FastAgent (agent/codeact/) - XML tool-calling agent, default mode
ManagerAgent (agent/manager/) - planning agent for reasoning mode
ExecutorAgent (agent/executor/) - action agent for reasoning mode
PortalClient (tools/android/portal_client.py) - HTTP client to on-device Portal
services: Portal APK (com.droidrun.portal) - on-device HTTP:8080 + Accessibility Service
MCP server (mcp_server.py) - phone_do/ping/apps over stdio JSON-RPC

api_clients.name: PortalClient
api_clients.protocol: HTTP (via ADB forward) or ADB content provider
api_clients.purpose: Send device actions, receive UI state

api_clients.name: MCPClientManager
api_clients.purpose: Consume external MCP tool servers as agent capabilities

api_clients.name: GitHub API / ungh.cc
api_clients.purpose: Download Portal APK from droidrun/droidrun-portal releases

secrets_references: DROIDRUN_DEEPSEEK_KEY (Windows env, from Bitwarden SM secret 14d69c11-...)
DROIDRUN_OPENROUTER_KEY (Windows env, from Bitwarden SM secret f9ed80a7-...)
BWS_ACCESS_TOKEN (Bitwarden Secrets Manager machine account token, bootstrapped from BWS_DROIDRUN_TOKEN in regular vault)

integrations.name: ADB (async_adbutils)
integrations.purpose: Android device connection, command execution, file transfer, port forwarding

integrations.name: DroidRun Portal APK
integrations.purpose: On-device HTTP API (port 8080) + Accessibility Service for UI tree and input injection

integrations.name: Tailscale
integrations.purpose: Secure remote ADB access without public internet exposure; device at 100.71.228.18

integrations.name: Bitwarden Secrets Manager
integrations.purpose: API key injection at startup; machine account access via bws CLI

integrations.name: MCP protocol (mcp library)
integrations.purpose: Both server (expose phone tools) and client (consume external tools)

integrations.name: Cursor/OpenClaw/Claude Desktop
integrations.purpose: Consume DroidRun MCP tools for phone automation from AI chat interfaces

integrations.name: Arize Phoenix / Langfuse
integrations.purpose: LLM trace export for debugging agent behavior

integrations.name: mobilerun-sdk
integrations.purpose: Unknown / Needs Verification - likely DroidRun Cloud device service

risks: llama-index pinned to 0.14.4 — outdated, may have security issues
No CI/CD pipeline — no automated testing or security scanning
Portal accessibility service may not survive Android reboot
ADB wireless debug port randomizes on reboot (mitigated by adb tcpip 5555)
mcp_server.py is workspace-specific, not synced with upstream droidrun
iOS driver is experimental stub, not production-ready
Trajectory files contain device screen content unencrypted on disk
LLM prompts include device UI content (may be sent to cloud LLM APIs)
unknowns: iOS driver actual functionality and stability
ToolRegistry class exact contents and usage
Portal APK HTTP API exact endpoint schema
Upstream droidrun CI/CD pipeline (not present in this workspace)
Auto-setup behavior on version mismatch (reinstall vs update)
